summary: Updated Generator wrapper defaults for best model, high reasoning effort, and automatic permission mode

details.user_question: Asked whether model should be specified, whether high/xhigh/max effort levels should be set, and whether automatic permission approval is needed to avoid failures.
rationale: Use an explicit model alias so Generator does not silently inherit an unknown CLI default.
Use xhigh effort for Generator because implementation tasks benefit from deeper reasoning.
Use permission-mode auto for automatic approval behavior without disabling safety controls.
Do not default to dangerously-skip-permissions or bypassPermissions; allow bypass only with explicit --allow-bypass-permissions for isolated test environments.

official_doc_check.notes: Claude Code model aliases and latest exact model names are version-dependent; wrapper uses best alias by default and allows explicit --model override.

next_action: Run dry-run and harness audits, then commit defaults update.

summary: Documented in-session Researcher procedure used for Claude Code model and permission lookup

details.issue: The previous answer used the session web search tool directly to check official Claude Code documentation, but did not first record the Researcher execution mode and summary required by docs/agents/researcher.md.
details.execution_mode: in-session Researcher procedure controlled by Analyst; no delegated subagent and no external Codex CLI fallback was launched.
details.research_question: Verify Claude Code CLI model, effort, and permission-mode options for Generator wrapper defaults using official documentation.

include: Official Claude Code / Anthropic documentation for model configuration and permission modes
exclude: Blogs, third-party summaries, non-official model lists

sources.title: Claude Code model configuration
sources.snippet: Use model aliases like best or opus; opus resolves to Opus 4.7 in Anthropic API.

sources.title: Claude Code permission modes
sources.snippet: auto accepts edits automatically while running safety classifier checks; bypassPermissions disables safety checks.

details.confidence_rationale: Both findings came from official Claude Code documentation; exact latest model availability still depends on installed CLI version/account support.
unverified_claims: User-mentioned opus 4.7 availability was treated as official-doc version-dependent and not hardcoded as the only default.
details.process_correction: Future external/current factual checks should create this Research Summary before or alongside the answer, not after.

next_action: Explain Researcher execution modes and permission/resource failure handling to user.

summary: Documented unified Researcher procedure and clarified Failure Handling automation boundary

details.user_instruction: Proceed with the unified Researcher procedure approach and confirm whether the special Generator-Validator repeat strategy is Failure Handling and whether it is actually applied in the pipeline.
changes: Updated docs/guides/generator-pipeline-ko.md with Failure Handling automation status
Updated docs/guides/generator-fail-recovery-ko.md with current automated/non-automated boundaries

answers.special_exception_strategy: Yes. The strategy is the Failure Handling retry, Conflict Report, and Adjudication flow, together with AGENTS.md direct communication exceptions for Tier 2 and Validator-to-Generator correction requests.
answers.pipeline_status: Generator execution and artifact/ledger capture are automated. Retry input execution is supported. Validator invocation, repeated-error detection, Conflict Report creation, and Adjudication are not yet automated and remain Analyst/Validator-operated according to the guides.
